title: AANA Agent Action Contract v1
description: Public seven-field pre-execution contract every agent or tool runtime can emit before a consequential action. AANA consumes this contract to decide whether the action should be accepted, clarified, deferred, or refused.

route_semantics.accept: execute only within the checked scope
route_semantics.ask: request missing authorization, confirmation, evidence, or clarification
route_semantics.defer: route to stronger evidence retrieval, domain owner, or human review
route_semantics.refuse: do not execute because a hard blocker prevents safe action

versioning_rules: v1 required fields must not be removed, renamed, reordered, or made optional in a v1-compatible schema.
Future v2/v3 schemas may add optional fields but must keep accepting valid v1 events unless an explicit new schema id is negotiated.
New route or enum values must be opt-in and old v1 clients should fail closed when they encounter them.
Result envelopes may add optional metadata, but gate_decision, recommended_action, hard_blockers, and audit-safe route information must remain backward-compatible.

schema_version.description: Optional compatibility marker for runtimes that use the original pre-tool-check wire name. The public v1 minimum is the seven required action fields.

tool_name.description: Runtime tool/function name exactly as the agent intends to call it.

tool_category.description: Public/non-sensitive reads may be accepted without identity auth. Private reads and writes require stronger authorization evidence.

authorization_state.description: Highest verified authorization state known before execution.

evidence_refs.description: References to evidence used to classify the tool call and authorization state. Keep raw private data out of this field when possible.

summary.description: Short redacted summary of what this evidence establishes.

retrieved_at.description: Optional ISO timestamp for freshness checks.

freshness.description: Freshness status used before public audit logs or claims.

checked_at.description: Optional ISO timestamp for when freshness was checked.

source.description: Redacted source of the freshness assertion.

provenance.description: Required redacted provenance label such as connector, policy, user, runtime, or fixture.

supports.description: Optional redacted claim/action ids this evidence supports.

contradicts.description: Optional redacted claim/action ids this evidence contradicts.

risk_domain.description: Primary risk surface for the proposed action.

proposed_arguments.description: Tool arguments the agent intends to pass. Redact secrets and private values where possible.

recommended_route.description: Runtime's proposed route before AANA applies the final gate.

request_id.description: Optional event/request id for audit correlation.

user_intent.description: Short redacted summary of the user request that led to this tool call.

authorization_subject.description: Optional redacted subject whose authorization was checked.
